name: Network Traffic Content
description: The full packet capture (PCAP) or session data that logs both protocol headers and payload content. This allows analysts to inspect command and control (C2) traffic, exfiltration, and other suspicious activity within network communications. Unlike metadata-based logs, full content analysis enables deeper protocol inspection, payload decoding, and forensic investigations.

tools.name: Wireshark / tcpdump / tshark
tools.description: Full packet captures (PCAP files) for manual analysis or IDS correlation.

tools.name: Zeek (formerly Bro)
tools.description: Extracts protocol headers and payload details into structured logs.
tools.command: echo "redef Log::default_store = Log::ASCII;" > local.zeek | zeek -Cr capture.pcap local.zeek

tools.name: Suricata / Snort
tools.description: Deep packet inspection (DPI) with signature-based and behavioral analysis.

sysmon.description: DNS Query Logging; captures DNS requests made by processes.
Network Connection Initiated; logs process-to-network connection relationships.

audit_framework.description: Monitors outbound network requests from processes.

cloud_ids.description: Detects malicious activity by analyzing network traffic patterns.
